<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Flashblade](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3465)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 128

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Nodachi](/Weapons.aspx?ID=298)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A sleek and impossibly lightweight blade attached to a haft scriven with lightning bolts make up this +[1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [nodachi](/Weapons.aspx?ID=298).

**Activate—Prepare Flash** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes;   **Effect** You concentrate with complete focus on your surroundings, breathing in the flashblade’s magic to make you lighter and faster, and the blade of your sword more flexible. You [Ready](/Actions.aspx?ID=2299) a melee Strike, increasing the flashblade’s reach for that Strike to 15 feet. If the trigger doesn’t occur, this doesn’t count against the flashblade’s frequency.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Heavenly Rolling Flames](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3466)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 129

**Price** 4,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand each

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Feng Huo Lun](/Weapons.aspx?ID=283)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This set of +[2](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2838) [feng huo lun](/Weapons.aspx?ID=283) is warm to the touch and wreathed in the bright red and orange glow of a constantly flickering flame. While wielded, you gain [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318)  2, and you treat the effects of [environmental cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=2814)  as one degree lower. Heavenly rolling flames can be Activated only if you wield two of them, and Activating them counts against the frequency for both weapons.

**Activate—Rolling Flight** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You attach the wheels to your feet, and their constant movement and energy allow you to move through the air. You gain a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) of 20 feet for 10 minutes. While in use in this way, you can’t wield the wheels as weapons. You can spend an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action at any time to end this effect and re-grip the heavenly rolling flames. If you lack enough free hands to wield them, you drop them in your space.

**Activate—Speed of Heaven** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_haste_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553) as a 3rd-rank [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) spell, but you can target only yourself.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Heavenly Rolling Flames

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Jian of Life's Duality](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3467)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 129

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=386)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This +[2](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=386) sports no extravagant characteristics save for a blade of pure, unblemished ivory and a hilt of unmarred obsidian. It takes on the propensity of its user, turning whiter or darker based on their actions. 

The effects of life's duality are based on the number and type of charges it has. It begins with 0 charges and can gain either dark or light charges, up to a maximum of 3 charges of one type. A charge dissipates after 10 minutes or when a charge of the opposite type is gained. If you have a charge of one type and would gain the opposite type of charge, you lose your current charges. 

Life's duality gains a dark charge when it critically succeeds at a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against an opponent or reduces the Hit Points of a living enemy to 0. For each dark charge it has, it deals an additional 1d4 [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage (maximum 3d4). 

Life's duality gains a light charge when you use a spell, skill check, or ability to restore at least 10 Hit Points to an ally. The damage healed must have been inflicted by a significant foe or threat. For each light charge it has, you gain 1d4 temporary Hit Points at the end of each round (maximum 3d4).

**Activate—Burst of Duality** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))   **Requirements** Life’s duality has 3 dark charges or 3 light charges; Frequency once per day; **Effect** You unleash the sword’s charged energy in a magical display. You cast a 3-action 5th-rank [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) spell if you have 3 dark charges or a 3-action 5th-rank [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) spell if you have 3 light charges (DC 30). The sword then reverts to having 0 charges.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Jian of Life's Duality

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Kotodama Bells](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3468)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 129

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This set of bronze bells bear engravings with symbols representing the word “awaken.” Among kotodama magic users, who seek to touch the souls of objects using the power of words, the peals of these bells are known to awaken the spirits of even inanimate objects.

**Activate—Awaken the Soul** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You ring the bell and focus on an object of negligible Bulk within 10 feet. The bell’s toll animates the object for 24 hours. The object becomes a [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=653) that can’t attack but can move and take simple [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions appropriate for an item of its type, as determined by the GM. For example, a piece of chalk can write a message or draw a symbol, a hand fan can open or fan in a particular direction, or a magnifying glass can set itself up to dramatically reveal incriminating evidence. The animated item has AC 5, 5 Hit Points, a Speed of 10 feet, and automatically fails all saves. If the object is [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60), it can no longer move, though it can still Interact as appropriate for an item of its type. As normal with minions you control, you must Command the object to grant it actions.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Kotodama Bells

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Kotodama Whistle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3469)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 129

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This small wooden whistle bears an engraving with a symbol representing the word “query.” Practitioners of kotodama magic use these whistles to speak with the spirits of objects.

**Activate—Question the Soul** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You place your hand on an object and play a single note on the whistle, causing the item to stir and respond to your questions for 1 minute. During this time, the object attempts to answer your questions to the best of its ability but can provide an answer of only “yes” or “no.” In most cases, an object has knowledge only of events it was personally present for and has no particular knowledge skills to interpret the events it has seen. If the object can’t answer a question with a simple yes or no answer, it stays silent.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Kotodama Whistle

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Peachwood Talisman](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3471)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=709" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 130, [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 20

**Price** 40 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Symbols of good fortune and luck are carved on this thin, square wooden plaque. It smells of sandalwood from the blessings placed upon it. After activation, for the next minute , you can sense attacks from [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722). You aren't [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79), [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96), or [flanking](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375), undead of your level or lower, or undead of your level or lower using [surprise attack](/Classes.aspx?ID=37). However, they can still help their allies flank.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Peachwood Talisman

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Peachwood Weapon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3472)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 130, [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 20

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** varies by weapon

**Bulk**  varies by weapon
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Peachwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3470)

</column>

---

Peachwood weapons have an auburn tinge in direct sunlight. Peachwood is treated as duskwood for [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creatures' [resistances](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318)  or [weaknesses](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) related to duskwood (bypassing a [jiang-shi's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1909) resistance, for example). In addition, peachwood weapons bypass a small portion of the resistances of any undead creature. [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with a peachwood weapon treat an undead's resistances against physical damage as 2 lower for standard-grade peachwood, and 4 lower for high-grade.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Peachwood Weapon (Standard-Grade)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 129, [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 20

**Price** 2,000 gp (+ 200 gp per bulk)

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

**Craft Requirements** At least 250 gp of peachwood + 25 gp per Bulk

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Peachwood Weapon (High-Grade)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 129, [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 20

**Price** 19,000 gp (+ 1,900 gp per Bulk)

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

**Craft Requirements** At least 9,500 gp of peachwood + 950 gp per Bulk
name: Peachwood Weapon

    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sun Herald's Stylus](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3473)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 130

**Price** 4,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This writing instrument was made from the spur of one of the giant divine roosters that heralds the presence of [Shizuru](/Deities.aspx?ID=315). While you hold it, you gain a +2 item bonus to Calligraphy Lore and [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) checks.

**Activate—Rejuvenating Ink** <actions string="Two Actions" /> (manipulate) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Drawing a circle on the ground with your stylus, you cast [_field of life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1526) centered on yourself.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sun Herald's Stylus

**Base Weapon** [Trident](/Weapons.aspx?ID=401)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The tiger fork is a +[1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [trident](/Weapons.aspx?ID=401) with the [disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577) and [grapple](/Traits.aspx?ID=619) traits. It has wide, flaring prongs that can be used to fend off deadly beasts and entrap opponents during combat. While you have another creature [Grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) with the tiger fork, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saves against [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=2364) effects.

**Activate—Fork Grip** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** Your last action was a successful Strike against a creature; **Effect** You can activate the tiger fork to attempt to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) the creature. This attempt uses the same multiple attack penalty as the required Strike.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tiger Fork

This +[2](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [impactful](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1062) [sansetsukon](/Weapons.aspx?ID=302) is made of metal colored pale blue that echoes howling winds when swung and emits the force of a storm when it connects.

**Activate—Sonic Gale** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Trigger** You critically hit with the twisting gale;  **Effect** The energy of your follow-through erupts from your opponent in a wave of concussive energy. You deal [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage equal to your normal [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) damage with the twisting gale in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) behind your target. Creatures in the area, not including your target, must attempt a basic Fortitude save against your class DC.

    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"

[Wand of Purification](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3476)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=731" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 130

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This cypress onusa rod is decorated with a number of paper streamers that rustle when shaken to direct purification magic. Wands of purification contain either [_cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467), [_clear mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1469), or [_sound body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1679), decided when the wand is created.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast cleanse affliction, clear mind, or sound body of the indicated level. If your [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) check would be sufficient only to suppress the effect until the beginning of your next turn, instead of to fully counteract it, then you can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the Activation of the wand each round to suppress the effect for an additional round, to a maximum of 1 minute. You Sustain the Activation by shaking the wand, so if at any point you release or otherwise drop the wand, the effect immediately stops being suppressed and resumes on the target as normal.

</column>

</row>

---

This cypress onusa rod is decorated with a number of paper streamers that rustle when shaken to direct purification magic. Wands of purification contain either [_cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467), [_clear mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1469), or [_sound body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1679), decided when the wand is created.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast cleanse affliction, clear mind, or sound body of the indicated level. If your [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) check would be sufficient only to suppress the effect until the beginning of your next turn, instead of to fully counteract it, then you can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the Activation of the wand each round to suppress the effect for an additional round, to a maximum of 1 minute. You Sustain the Activation by shaking the wand, so if at any point you release or otherwise drop the wand, the effect immediately stops being suppressed and resumes on the target as normal.

The Bow of Sun Slaying is a [+3](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [greater frost](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2839) [composite shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=435) constructed out of wood, horn, and sinew. It bears carvings of a long-forgotten demigod who legends say possessed the ability to destroy the sun with a single arrow.

**Activate—Darkness for My Foes** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** Your last action was a successful [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against your foe with this weapon; **Effect** You whisper, “Darkness for my foes,” and the target of your last attack takes 10d6 [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) damage and 10d6 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage (DC 45 basic Will save).

</column>

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=537" />

---

The Frost Fair Yanyuedao is a [+2](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) yanyuedao (use statistics for [glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=375)) once wielded by a legendary military general from Goka. This weapon was constructed from pieces harvested from a dragon’s body. When in an area of [severe cold or colder](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2820), the Frost Fair Yanyuedao becomes a +3 major striking yanyuedao with a glowing blue cutting edge and the following ability.

**Activate—Dragon Chill** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** Until the end of your turn, the Frost Fair Yanyuedao gains the effects of a greater [frost](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2839) rune. While under this effect, if you critically succeed at a Strike using this weapon against a creature who has [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) or [immunity](/Rules.aspx?ID=2313) to [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), that creature must attempt a DC 31 Fortitude save or be [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Frost Fair Yanyuedao

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Navaratna of the Solar Ruby](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3480)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=723" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=537" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 131

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These nine flawless gems are set within a golden necklace, their centerpiece a holy ruby pulled from the center of a sun. So long as you are wearing the navaratna, you ignore all [environmental effects due to temperature](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820), do not take penalties due to [wind](/Rules.aspx?ID=2821), and ignore all damage and effects from droughts, [floods](/Rules.aspx?ID=2832), and [earthquakes](/Rules.aspx?ID=2828). You are also immune to damage from [starvation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2603). The navaratna does not otherwise grant you resistance against damage (such as [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) or [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) damage).

**Activate—Sutra of the Flawless Servant** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per hour;; **Trigger** An ally within 30 feet fails a saving throw against an [temperature](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820) environmental effect;  **Effect** You offer a prayer for your divine protection to extend to your companion. The creature rerolls the triggering saving throw with a +2 item bonus. They must take the new result, even if it is worse.

**Destruction** The navaratna must be swallowed by a fiendish [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) [lion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2866); the lion must then by killed by drowning in a lake of [naga](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=446) venom and left to rot for a year. At the stroke of midnight on the final day, the artifact is destroyed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Navaratna of the Solar Ruby

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Staff of Sun Wukong](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3481)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=723" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=537" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 131

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Bo Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=372)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Also known as _Ruyi Bang_, this [+3](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [greater extending](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2836) [grievous](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2841) [bo staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=372) is a legendary artifact wielded by the Monkey King, [Sun Wukong](/Deities.aspx?ID=316). Unlike normal bo staves, the Staff of Sun Wukong is made of solid iron with two brilliant gold bands at either end.

**Activate—Meteor Slam** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You extend the staff with the force of a meteor, dealing 10d10 bludgeoning damage to a single creature within 120 feet and making it [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 1 day. All other creatures within 10 feet of the target (except the staff’s wielder) take 8d10 [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage (DC 43 basic Reflex save).

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> Close at Hand;     **Effect** It’s said that Sun Wukong tucked this magical staff behind his ear during his travels. When not in use, the Staff of Sun Wukong shrinks to the size and weight of a needle or toothpick, making it highly concealable (+4 circumstance bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks to hide it from sight). In this form, the item has negligible Bulk. You can [Activate](/Actions.aspx?ID=100) this ability again to return the staff to its normal size.

**Activate—Cloud Somersault** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You use the shapeshifting capabilities of this relic to propel yourself great distances. You [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) up to 50 feet in any direction.

**Destruction** Despite being made of iron and gold, mundane heat sources can’t damage the Staff of Sun Wukong. Magical fire similarly has no effect on this relic. The only way to destroy this item is to expose it to the intense heat of a specific volcano in the depths of the Songil Sea (though which volcano remains shrouded in myth).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Staff of Sun Wukong

These fermented and dried tea leaves are rolled into a ball shaped like a pearl. When brewed as a tea and consumed, it promotes an outpouring of vital energy that surges through your body. For the next 10 minutes, you have resistance 15 to void damage, and your unarmed attacks deal an additional 1d6 points of vitality damage on a successful Strike. While this effect is active, whenever a damaging attack or effect would reduce you to 0 Hit Points, you can use your reaction to immediately end the benefits of dragon pearl and remain conscious and standing with 10 Hit Points, increasing your [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) condition by 1.

</column>

**Activate—Blood Revitalization** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You would take persistent bleed damage;  **Effect** You don’t take the persistent bleed damage and instead regain Hit Points equal to the bleed damage. The persistent bleed damage ends.

Made from the finest plates of monstrous insect chitin, this _+2 greater resilient [mantis shell](/Armor.aspx?ID=30)_ offers superior protection from Mediogalti's hot weather while also allowing the wearer to blend into almost any situation and strike with hidden blades. While wearing this armor, you are protected from extreme heat and severe heat effects. Vernai shell armor includes two extradimensional spaces built into each of the armor's gloves, granting the wearer two places to store items. Each glove can hold one item of 1 Bulk or less. While an item is stored in one of the two gloves, there is nothing to indicate that an item is being held inside it. 

**Activate—Store Item** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))   **Requirements** At least one of the _Vernai shell's_ two extradimensional spaces is empty; **Effect** One item you’re holding with a Bulk of 1 or less vanishes into one of the armor’s extradimensional spaces. 

**Activate—Retrieve Item** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))   **Requirements** An item is stored in at least one of the _Vernai shell's_ extradimensional spaces, and you have a free hand.; **Effect** One item of your choice in either extradimensional space appears in your hand. You can’t Retrieve Item again for 1 minute

This _+3 greater striking [keen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2843) [sawtooth saber](/Weapons.aspx?ID=423)_ has a black blade that always seems freshly smeared with blood. 

**Activate—Drink Blood** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))  **Trigger** You reduce a creature to 0 Hit Points with the weapon;  **Effect** You gain a number of temporary Hit Points equal to twice the creature's level. If you're also wielding _Fleshrender_ in your other hand, you instead gain temporary Hit Points equal to three times the creature's level. These Hit Points remain for 1 minute. 

**Activate—Wall of Sabers** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per hour;  **Requirements** _Fleshrender_ is within 30 feet and isn't currently held by you; **Effect** A whirling wall of sawtooth sabers appear in the air between the paired weapons. All creatures in a line between you and _[Fleshrender](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3488)_ take 10d6 slashing damage and 4d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) damage (DC 38 basic Reflex save).

Carved from the mandible of a giant praying mantis, this magical weapon's blade is as sharp and serviceable as steel. 

**Activate—Sweeping Slash** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You swing the dagger in a wide arc, causing a horizontal swipe of blood- red energy to sweep out in a 30-foot cone. Favored by Red Mantis assassins who enjoy using illusions to confound their targets, a crimson bluff constantly flickers and flashes, creating brief afterimages that can be quite distracting. If you use a crimson bluff as part of a gesture when you [Create a Diversion](/Actions.aspx?ID=2387), you gain a +2 item bonus to your Deception check.

The triggering Strike uses the outcome for one degree of success worse than the result of the attack roll. The duration of your _blur_ effect is reduced by 2 rounds.

    right="Item 16"
    pfs=""
>
[Temperbrand](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3489)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Prey for Death</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=723" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Prey for Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=231) pg. 102

**Price** 7,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Maul](/Weapons.aspx?ID=388)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When the mighty fire-and-metal elemental Temperbrand is defeated, their corpse transforms into a _+3 major striking [greater flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2838) [grievous](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2841) [shifting](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2846) [maul](/Weapons.aspx?ID=388)_ made of what appears to be molten steel, though it behaves as if it were made of solid metal. When a creature first picks up Temperbrand, they must succeed at a DC 43 Will save to establish their dominance over the weapon. On a success, they become Temperbrand's only accepted wielder and may use the weapon normally. On a failure, the character takes 20d6 fire damage (40d6 fire damage on a critical failure) and drops the weapon as it melts away into slag and immediately reforms in an adjacent space. A new attempt to establish dominance can be made by picking the weapon up again and attempting a new Will save. Once Temperbrand has an accepted wielder, it can't be wielded by anyone else unless the accepted wielder intentionally permits it, declaring so in a loud voice; this permission can be rescinded in a similar manner. Anyone else trying to wield Temperbrand after it has accepted a wielder takes fire damage and causes the weapon to melt away as if they failed the Will save to wield it.

 The additional fire damage caused by Temperbrand's greater flaming property (but not its persistent fire damage) is splash damage. Temperbrand's wielder is immune to this splash damage. 

**Activate—Molten Smash** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You smash Temperbrand against the ground or another solid adjacent surface, causing an explosion of molten metal and force to radiate outward in a 20-foot emanation. All creatures in this area take 10d6 fire damage and 6d6 force damage (DC 43 basic Reflex save); those who critically fail this save are knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). You are immune to the effects of Molten Smash, but your allies are not.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Temperbrand

This thick, blood-red elixir sharpens your senses and makes you more acutely aware of blood. You gain blood sight with the listed duration and effects. Blood sight has a range of 60 feet and allows you to detect living creatures who are taking [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) damage or who have the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) or [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) conditions. You also detect free-standing puddles or droplets of recently spilled blood. At the GM's discretion, living creatures without blood can't be detected with your blood sight.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs=""
>
[Breath of the Mantis God](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3491)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Prey for Death</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Inhaled" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=634" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=669" />
<trait label="Virulent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=726" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Prey for Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=231) pg. 103

**Price** 200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

To prevent one of their victims from being brought back to life, Red Mantis assassins often poison targets with the breath of the mantis god. After a creature is poisoned by this concoction, internal hemorrhaging frequently results in blood issuing from the creature's mouth—a condition referred to by the assassins as having “the breath of the mantis god.” While a creature can attempt to recover normally from the persistent bleed damage caused by breath of the mantis god, the persistent bleed damage will return if the poison's duration is still ongoing. If a creature dies from the poison's effects, the toxin lingers tenaciously in the creature's flesh for 1 year. During this time, if an attempt is made to bring such a slain creature back to life that doesn't create a new body for the deceased (such as with a 7th-rank [_resurrect_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=123) ritual), the lingering effects of breath of the mantis god attempts to counteract the resurrection ([counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) modifier +17, counteract rank 5). A spell like [_extract poison_](/Spells.aspx?ID=907) can be used to decontaminate a corpse for easier resurrection, but simpler magic such as cleanse cuisine cannot. A 5th-rank or higher cleanse affliction can also attempt to counteract lingering breath of the mantis god.

 **Saving Throw** DC 29 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 minutes; **Stage 1** 3d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (1 minute); **Stage 2** 3d8 persistent bleed and drained 1 (1 minute); **Stage 3** 3d10 persistent bleed and drained 2 (1 minute)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Breath of the Mantis God

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs=""
>
[Crimson Tome](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3492)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Prey for Death</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=629" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Prey for Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=231) pg. 103

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These tomes are sacred to the Red Mantis assassins, who use them to scribe their spells and record their violent deeds. The first of them were created in Rahadoum, but when the Oath Wars forced the worshippers of Achaekek to flee Rahadoum, many were identified by their _crimson tomes_, which were destroyed on the spot. Less than a dozen volumes survived, and the secret of creating them has been lost to history. Rumors persist of a first edition of this rare tome—an artifact version of the one presented here, but if these rumors are true, even the Red Mantis have lost track of such a treasure.

 Each _crimson tome_ is bound in fine red leather with gold filigree on the cover and golden edge gilding. When an [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629) spell is prepared from this grimoire, it becomes more difficult to purposefully disbelieve. Creatures that attempt to disbelieve an illusion spell prepared from a crimson tome must roll their check twice and take the worse result; this is a misfortune effect. This does not affect the saving throws of spells with the illusion trait that call for a saving throw to resolve effects other than disbelief, such as with [_dizzying colors_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1500) or [_vision of death_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742).

**Activate—Crimson Focusing** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You have fewer Focus Points than your maximum number of Focus Points; **Effect** You draw on the _crimson tome's_ energies and regain one Focus Point; the _crimson tome's_ leather binding turns black until your next daily preparations.

**Activate—Achaekek's Gift** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513))  **Trigger** You kill a creature with a spell prepared from the _crimson tome_;  **Effect** The _crimson tome_ infuses the slain creature's mortal remains and soul. If an effect attempts to restore the slain creature to life, the _crimson tome_ immediately attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) that effect (counteract modifier +27, counteract rank 8). If the restoring effect is counteracted, you immediately learn the name, nature, and location of the creature or effect that attempted to bring the dead body back to life. You can only impart Achaekek's Gift on one creature at a time. If you use this ability on another creature, the previous creature loses Achaekek's Gift.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Crimson Tome

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs=""
>
[Elysian Clairglass](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3493)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Prey for Death</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Scrying" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=689" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Prey for Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=231) pg. 103

**Price** 14,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Used by soldiers who battle on the borderlands of Elysium, particularly in Gorum’s realm of Clashing Shore, these bronze spyglasses are somewhat plain-looking save for a single diamond set into the largest cylinder. An Elysian clairglass functions like a typical spyglass, but you can see twenty times farther while looking through it, rather than eight times farther. It also grants a +3 item bonus to Perception checks to notice details at a distance.

**Activate—Seek and Speak** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _Elysian clairglass_ casts [_clairaudience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1465) and [_clairvoyance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466) on the area you are currently observing with it. As long as you're holding the _Elysian clairglass_, you can speak through the floating eye or ear created by its spells, causing your voice to manifest as a whisper that only one person in range of the spells can hear, as a booming voice that everyone in range of the spells can hear, or as any combination in between those two extremes.

**Activate—Pinpoint** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_pinpoint_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1633).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Elysian Clairglass

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs=""
>
[Eye of the Mantis](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3494)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Prey for Death</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Prey for Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=231) pg. 104

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This 1-inch tall figurine depicts a crouched praying mantis. It’s usually carved from green jade, crimson marble, or purple porphyry. Regardless of the figurine’s material composition, its gemstone eyes are always a dull ruby red that seems to drink light.

**Activate—Phantasmal Assassin** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast 4th-rank [_vision of death_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742) (DC 28). To the target, it always appears as if they are being attacked by an immense praying mantis.

**Activate—Scouting Eye** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_scouting eye_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1661).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Eye of the Mantis

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Assassin's Bracers](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3495)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Prey for Death</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=629" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Prey for Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=231) pg. 104

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn bracers

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These paired bracers of red leather appear as plain armbands on the outside but display the branded symbol of [Achaekek](/Deities.aspx?ID=325) on the inside. They grant the wearer a +1 item bonus to AC and saving throws, and a maximum Dexterity modifier of +5 as armor. You can affix talismans to _assassin's bracers_ as though they were light armor.

**Activate—Focus on the Doomed** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You designate one creature within 30 feet. You cast 4th-rank [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) on yourself, but the designated creature can still see you. The bracers enhance your appearance to the designated creature, making you appear more fearsome and unnerving, granting you a +1 item bonus to attempts to Feint or Demoralize the designated creature. The designated creature takes a –1 item penalty to saving throws against fear effects.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Mantis Embrace](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3496)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Prey for Death</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Prey for Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=231) pg. 104

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These stylized red gloves are constructed from plates of leather that have been treated to resemble insect chitin. The gloves grant the wearer a +2 item bonus to Athletics checks to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) or [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380).

**Activate—Crushing Embrace** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per hour;  **Requirements** You are grappling a creature; **Effect** You Strike the creature you are grappling with a melee weapon or unarmed attack. This Strike deals an additional 3d6 precision damage and gains the death trait. The body of a creature that is slain by Crushing Embrace is so gruesomely damaged that the creature's body cannot be affected by any effect that requires an intact body to function, such as the [_talking corpse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712) spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Limited"
>
Mantis Embrace
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Prey for Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=231) pg. 104

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Limited"
>
Mantis Embrace (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Prey for Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=231) pg. 104

**Price** 13,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus to Athletics is +3, and the additional precision damage is 5d6.
name: Mantis Embrace

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Mask of the Mantis](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3497)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Prey for Death</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Prey for Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=231) pg. 104

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn head

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The traditional headwear for the Red Mantis assassin is the _mask of the mantis_. Most of these masks are constructed from hard leather helmets that serve to obscure the assassin's identity and give them the appearance of possessing a mantis's head. Some variants, however, consist only of the mask itself, and are meant to be worn over the face. Regardless of the shape and style, all _masks of the mantis_ function the same.

 Members of the Vernai can use any mask of the mantis to enhance their frightening presence—something they often do simply to reveal their standing in the society without speaking a word, as detailed fully in the entry for Vernai's Ire activation below.

 While wearing a _mask of the mantis_, you gain a +1 item bonus to Perception checks.

**Activate—Enhance Vision** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast either [_darkvision_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1481) or [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) on yourself.

**Activate—Vernai's Ire** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))   **Requirements** You are a member of the Vernai; **Effect** You cause the mask of the mantis's eyes to shimmer with crimson light, granting yourself a far more harrowing visage. This light creates illumination equivalent to that of a candle. You can use this ability again to extinguish the frightening glow, but as long as Vernai's Ire remains active, you gain a +1 item bonus to Intimidation checks.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Limited"
>
Mask of the Mantis
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 150 gp

pfs="Limited"

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 1,350 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to Perception and Intimidation is +2. When you Enhance Vision, the _darkvision_ spell or _see the unseen_ spell is heightened to 5th-rank.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Limited"
>
Mask of the Mantis (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Prey for Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=231) pg. 104

**Price** 19,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to Perception and Intimidation is +3. 

**Activate—Locate Target** <actions string="Two Actions" /> 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_pinpoint_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1633). If the target of this spell is a creature for which you've accepted a contract to assassinate, you are considered to have seen the creature in person for the purposes of _pinpoint's_ requirements.
name: Mask of the Mantis

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mythic Armor Potency](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3498)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 148

**Price** 70,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This armor is etched with a mythical ward providing unparalleled defense. Increase the armor’s item bonus to AC by 4, and the armor can be etched with four [property runes](/Rules.aspx?ID=3168).

**Activate—Survive Devastation** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))  **Trigger** An enemy critically succeeds against you with a weapon or unarmed [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306);  **Effect** Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320); if the triggering Strike was made by a mythic creature, it’s a normal success instead. If it was made by a non-mythic creature, it’s a failure.

**Craft Requirements** Mythic armor potency runes can only be crafted, etched, or transferred by a mythic character  capable of making a [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) check at mythic proficiency (such as by having the Artisan’s Calling).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mythic Armor Potency

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mythic Resilent](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3499)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 149

**Price** 70,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Mythic resilient runes imbue armor with unrivaled protection from a wide array of effects. The armor grants a +4 item bonus to saving throws to the wearer.

**Activate—Defy Obliteration** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))  **Trigger** You critically fail a saving throw;  **Effect** Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320); if the triggering save was made due to an effect created by a mythic monster, hazard, or other effect, it’s a normal failure instead. If the save was made due to an effect that wasn’t mythic, it becomes a success.

**Craft Requirements** Mythic resilient runes can only be crafted, etched, or transferred by a mythic character capable of making a [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) check at mythic proficiency (such as by having the Artisan’s Calling).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mythic Resilent

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mythic Striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3500)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 149

**Price** 70,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This weapon is filled with unmatched destructive power. The weapon deals five weapon damage dice.

**Activate—Unstoppable Devastation** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))  **Trigger** You roll the weapon damage dice for a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with this weapon and do not like the result;  **Effect** Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) and reroll your weapon damage dice, taking the higher of the two results.

**Craft Requirements** Mythic striking runes can only be crafted, etched, or transferred by a mythic character capable of making a [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) check at mythic proficiency (such as by having the Artisan’s Calling).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mythic Striking

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 24"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragon-Lotus Drum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3507)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=723" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=537" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 150

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This traditional barrel drum has tacked heads in the old military style of Hwanggot and is painted with a many-colored imperial dragon twined around white and pink lotuses, a symbol of long memory. The dragon holds a scroll in its two front claws that bears the inscription “So that the memory of war may be truthfully recalled.” 

A creature who activates the drum is bound to join its army after death. Once their spirit has fought in a hundred and one battles, they are freed to face whatever fate their soul has earned. However, until this duty is discharged, the dead creature cannot be resurrected, nor can their soul be captured by any other means.

**Activate—Summon Dragon-Lotus Army** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [incarnate](/Traits.aspx?ID=786), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once every 20 years;   **Effect** Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320); beating the drum summons a spectral army to an area you designate within 500 feet. The army takes its Arrive action when you finish playing. At the end of your next turn, the army [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to 60 feet and then takes its Depart action. The Dragon-Lotus Army follows your orders and attempts to not harm you and your allies when possible. The spectral force isn’t fully a creature. It can’t take any other actions, nor can it be targeted or harmed by [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306), spells, or other effects unless they would be able to target or end a spell effect (such as [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493)). The army is Gargantuan, but it doesn’t block movement.

**Arrive** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) _The Sound of Thunder_ The beating of the drum amplifies to become like the stomping of hundreds of booted feet. The Dragon-Lotus Army appears in perfect formation and gives a brief, staccato battle cry. All enemies in a 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) around the army take 16d10 sonic damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) DC 48 Fortitude save); each creature who fails its save is also [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 3 (frightened 4 on a critical failure). 

**Depart** _Devastating Charge_ The army readies their motley collection of weapons and Strides forward up to 100 feet in a straight [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388). Each creature it moves through takes 20d8 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (whichever would be most harmful at the GM’s discretion) with a basic DC 48 Reflex save. A creature who critically fails its save is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).

**Destruction** If the Dragon-Lotus Drum is captured by an enemy of Hwanggot (such as a warring rival nation) and then played continually for 1 week while singing anthems that disparage Hwanggot and its leaders, the drum falls to pieces and the souls bound to it are obliterated.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragon-Lotus Drum

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Final Scalecloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3508)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=723" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=537" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 151

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Fashioned from a swath of Mogaru’s red-and-black hide and trimmed with Agyra’s rough scales in green, blue, and white, this cloak draws upon the power of both kaiju, but at considerable cost. Any [kaiju](/Traits.aspx?ID=383) within a 5-mile radius of the Final Scalecloak can sense the presence and nature of the item, and can also determine whether it’s being worn by a mortal. This sense grows stronger as the item and kaiju near one another, until the kaiju can pinpoint exactly where the cloak is and who currently possesses it.

When worn, the Final Scalecloak grants you [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 15 to physical and precision damage, [immunity](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), and a [Fly speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) of 60 feet. You are unaffected by strong winds while flying. 

**Activate—Commune with Kaiju** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320); you gain the effects of a 6th-rank [_telepathy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1719) spell but can use it only to communicate with Gargantuan [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) and [beasts](/Traits.aspx?ID=547). Most kaiju are [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=80) toward you for wearing the Final Scalecloak, however, requiring great effort to calm them enough to have a conversation.

**Activate—Embody the Storm** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320); a nimbus of crackling electricity surrounds you for 1 minute. You gain a +4 status bonus to AC against ranged projectiles that are at least partly made of metal. If a foe attempts to [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) you with an unarmed attack or melee attack with a weapon at least partly made of metal, that creature takes 3d10 [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) DC 40 Reflex save).

**Destruction** The Final Scalecloak is torn to shreds if the wearer is struck by an attack or effect from both Agyra and Mogaru within the same round.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Final Scalecloak

</title>

<traits>

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Morningstar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=363)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _[+4](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2842) [morningstar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=363)_ is made of wood and adorned with golden flames traced along its haft and head. It has the [razing](/Traits.aspx?ID=784) trait, and it ignores the [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) of structures used to hold those you feel are being unjustly imprisoned (subject to the GM’s discretion). 

**Activate—Break Free** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320); _Freedom’s Flame_ blazes with a rose-red light, creating a beacon of spiritual strength. Any creature within 120 feet that’s currently under a compulsion or [control](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64) effect can immediately attempt a new saving throw to break free of the effect, with a +3 status bonus.

**Activate—Holy Retribution** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** Your previous action was to use _Freedom’s Flame_ to [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) a foe you have witnessed use a compulsion or [control](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64) effect on an ally within the past hour;  **Effect** Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320); your blow is empowered by the righteousness of [Milani](/Deities.aspx?ID=43). The target’s [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) is triggered again or, if the target doesn’t have a holy weakness, it takes 15 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage.

**Destruction** _Freedom’s Flame_ is sliced in two if struck by a [final blade](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1094) that has been anointed with [unholy water](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3006) created by a level 20 [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) creature.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 23"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Shadowpiercer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3510)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=723" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=537" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 152

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=365)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Muted, silvery moonlight gleams along the curved blade of this _[+4](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) [spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=365)_. Braided tassels of horsehair dangle from its sturdy haft, and archaic runes in Nidalese script shimmer along the weapon in the presence of shadow magic.

_Shadowpiercer_ ignores [immunity](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) and [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to precision damage of creatures with the [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) or [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=693) trait, and deals an additional 2d6 precision damage to such creatures. While wielding _Shadowpiercer_, you gain a +2 status bonus to saving throws against [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=569) and shadow effects; this bonus also applies to allies within 60 feet of you while you wield the spear.

If you are a worshipper of [Zon-Kuthon](/Deities.aspx?ID=296), you are [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 4 and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 4 while carrying or wielding _Shadowpiercer_. If you use a darkness or shadow effect while carrying or wielding _Shadowpiercer_, you become enfeebled 4; this condition’s value reduces by 1 at the start of each day you do not have physical contact with _Shadowpiercer_.

**Activate—Absorb Shadows** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You touch _Shadowpiercer_ to a [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=569) or [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=693) effect to siphon its energy and bolster yourself. You gain a number of temporary Hit Points equal to four times the effect’s [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) rank. These temporary Hit Points last for 1 hour. In addition, if the effect is causing darkness, that darkness is automatically counteracted within 15 feet of you for as long as you have those temporary Hit Points. This doesn’t provide light—it merely restores the area to its natural illumination level.

**Activate—Rebuff Gloom** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** You don’t currently have the [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) condition; **Trigger** You or an ally within 60 feet is targeted by a [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=569) or [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=693) effect;  **Effect** Spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320); the spear drains your vitality to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the effect. You can attempt to counteract the effect (+46 modifier level, counteract rank 10). You can activate this effect without having a Mythic Point available but doing so makes you [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 2.

**Destruction** _Shadowpiercer_ simply disappears if a worshipper of [Zon-Kuthon](/Deities.aspx?ID=296) casts a 10th-rank [_darkness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) spell on it once a day for an entire month while it is unattended.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shadowpiercer

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 25"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[World Forge](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3511)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=723" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=537" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 152

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Mounted upon a base of iron, inscribed with innumerable runes, this immense anvil can be used to forge nearly anything into reality. Stories claim that even metaphorical and abstract concepts, including “a [graveknight’s](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=423) mercy” and “the tears of a [living wildfire](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2982)” have been hammered into solid shape upon _Worldforge_, though the tales disagree about what form those objects took.

The cost of using _Worldforge_, however, is prohibitive for mere mortals. In addition to the fuel and metal it consumes, _Worldforge_ draws upon the user’s physical and spiritual strength to sustain the act of creation. The demand of its magic is such that no one save gods, or those who are nearly gods, can use it and survive.

**Activate—Wondrous Forge** <actions string="" /> ([downtime](/Traits.aspx?ID=580), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** You have the ability to make a [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) check at mythic proficiency (such as that granted by the Artisan’s Calling); **Effect** After spending 2 days setting up the work (or 1 if you have the desired item’s formula) and supplying _Worldforge_ with adequate raw materials (as determined by the GM), you attempt to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) something upon it. The DC and final cost of the item is also determined by the GM. For each day you spend Crafting, you must expend a Mythic Point or your [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) condition increases by 1; this condition can’t be reduced until you have finished Crafting the item or you abandon the project. Each day spent Crafting upon the Worldforge counts as 10 days of normal Crafting time.

If the value of your drained condition reaches 4, you must attempt a DC 46 Fortitude save or [die](/Rules.aspx?ID=2329). If you fail this save or if you abandon the activity, what remains of your project shatters irrecoverably into a multitude of useless fragments.

**Destruction** _Worldforge_ can’t be destroyed as long as [Torag](/Deities.aspx?ID=294) maintains his divinity.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: World Forge

This _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [ogre hook](/Weapons.aspx?ID=495)_ causes its owner to dream of past murders conducted with the weapon. _Gut-Ripper's_ mythic magic is tied to the bloodlust of the [ogre boss](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3120) who was holding it upon becoming mythic. If _Gut-Ripper_ doesn't kill a creature each day by midnight, it becomes a non-magical ogre hook.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Rip Guts! Rip Guts!; **Cost** 1 Mythic Point;    **Effect** You throw _Gut-Ripper_ in a line of any length up to 60 feet, then it returns to your hands. Each creature in the line takes 2d10 piercing damage with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. If there's a creature at the end of the line, you instead make a melee Strike against it with the hook that deals an additional 2d10 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). Each time a creature takes this bleed damage, you regain that many HP; this is a [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) effect. This activation can't be used again for 1d4 rounds.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wandering Pipe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3513)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=723" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=537" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 184

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This long-stemmed pipe always emits a thin plume of smoke, even when not lit or in active use. The exact material the pipe is made of varies in every accounting; sometimes made of polished oak, other times ebony, sometimes stone, and in one Erutaki legend the pipe is made of pure ice that somehow holds a flame. Though the _wandering pipe_ is not intelligent, it is rumored to possess a certain capriciousness, leaving the possession of mortals whose lives are not exciting enough but inevitably finding its way back to its one true owner. 

When held in one hand, the pipe grants its holder a +2 circumstance bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks but imposes a -1 circumstance penalty to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks made to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405), as its telltale plume of smoke gives away the bearer's position.

**Activate—Smoky Protections** <actions string="Two Actions" /> (concentrate, manipulate, primal) **Cost** 1 Mythic Point; **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For the next 10 minutes, smoke gathers around you buoying your steps and protecting you. For the duration of this effect, you gain a fly Speed equal to your land Speed and automatically hover in place, and you have [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) from ranged attacks.

**Destruction** The _wandering pipe_ can be broken as easily as any other wooden pipe, but it always reforms afterward in the hands of the [Immortal Trickster](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3405) or one of his bonded mortals. It can only be broken by the hands of one of the Trickster's bonded mortals who is an honest person acting with good intent; it is said that if this occurs, the Trickster would also be significantly weakened as a result.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wandering Pipe

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Immaculate Instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3514)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=537" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 203

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn or held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This object, made of silver light, takes the form of a small musical instrument, prop, or other tool associated with a specific art form. The holder of the _immaculate instrument_ never suffers from creative blocks of any kind and their work is always insightful and skilled. A character who uses the _immaculate instrument_ to [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=2401) or [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) can attempt the check at mythic proficiency once per month, and as long as they possess their _immaculate instrument_, they treat any critical failures with these skills as failures.

**Destruction** If the holder uses the _immaculate instrument_ to intentionally produce a work that is not just mediocre or crass, but one that devalues the public opinion of the art form itself in a showing of at least 100 spectators, the instrument fades away in shame.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Immaculate Instrument
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 203

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Cursed Immaculate Instrument
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=567" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 203

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

This _immaculate instrument_ has been cursed by the [sublime breath](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3408) it was taken from, causing it to warp, rust, or tarnish, though the holder of the instrument cannot perceive this and rationalizes away any attempt to explain the instrument's obviously cursed nature. Whenever the _cursed immaculate instrument_ is used to produce a work, the holder must succeed on a DC 16 flat check or the work fails, leaving the holder frustrated and raising the DC of subsequent flat checks by 1.

 When the DC reaches 20, the work fails as normal, but the holder continues to attempt it fruitlessly to the exclusion of all other activities, including eating, drinking, and sleep, until they expire or the curse is broken.
name: Immaculate Instrument

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Shard of Self-Destruction](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3516)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=567" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 208

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This jagged shard of bone appears to be and functions as a _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358)_ with a sharp edge that is perpetually stained with blood. Whenever you critically hit with the weapon, you deal an additional 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), but you also take 1d6 persistent bleed damage. You take a –2 penalty to the flat check to remove this bleed damage, and when you succeed at this flat check, you are exposed to Verex's ruin (see below) as the site of the injury grows red and inflamed, your blood vessels discoloring and swelling as if serrated knives were trying to push their way out.

 Once the curse has activated for the first time, the weapon fuses to you; while you can sheathe the dagger outside of combat, it appears in your hand when a fight begins and you can't sheathe it as long as there is an enemy you can perceive.

 **Verex's Ruin** ([disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) **Saving Throw** DC 22 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 hour; **Stage 1** [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) (1 day); **Stage 2** enfeebled 2, [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), and 1d6 spirit damage each time you would take persistent bleed damage (1 week); **Stage 3** as stage 2, but the spirit damage increases to 2d6 (1 week); **Stage 4** enfeebled 3, fatigued, and 4d6 spirit damage each time you would take persistent bleed damage (1 week); **Stage 5** death

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shard of Self-Destruction

stage_markdown:
Stage 1: [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) (1 day)

Stage 2: enfeebled 2, [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), and 1d6 spirit damage each time you would take persistent bleed damage (1 week)

Stage 3: as stage 2, but the spirit damage increases to 2d6 (1 week)

Stage 4: enfeebled 3, fatigued, and 4d6 spirit damage each time you would take persistent bleed damage (1 week)

Stage 5: death

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 215 --- Made from the webs spun from the thorax of the [Weaver of Webs](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3410) herself, dreamweb offers a number of mystic benefits. Cloth items and rope made from dream web have Hardness 4 with 12 HP per inch and a break DC of 16. Light armor made from dream web is lighter than normal cloth or leather: the armor's Bulk is reduced by 1 (reduced to light Bulk if the armor's normal Bulk is 1, with no effect on an item that normally has light Bulk). Light armor made from dream web grants the wearer poison resistance equal to 3 plus the value of its [_armor potency_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2785) rune. ## Dreamweb

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[One-Hour Flower](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3518)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Convocation</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 29

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Popular with lovers, decorators, and sports fans, the seeds of these ephemeral plants grow and bloom immediately into flowers when placed in any warm environment, including a cup of dirt, a bowl of warm water, and a tightly clenched hand. The color of these flowers varies, but each has an internal glow that sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2401) in a 30-foot radius. The radius of this light shrinks by 10 feet every 20 minutes. At the end of an hour, the plant disintegrates, leaving only a vaguely pleasant scent in the air.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: One-Hour Flower

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spiced Demonade](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3519)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Convocation</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=589" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 29

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

There are some who claim that the original version of this tart red drink contained the ground skin of actual demons, but in truth, spiced demonade was created by a first-year academy student desperate to look awake and alert after a night of carousing.

 After consuming spiced demonade, you ignore all effects and penalties from the consumption of alcohol and lack of sleep for 1 hour. These effects resume when the spiced demonade wears off, and you become temporarily immune to spiced demonade for 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spiced Demonade
